audit_items.proof_trace: adev_release (L187) is registered as adev->dev.release (L216), meaning it is called by the device model when the kobject refcount reaches 0. Calling auxiliary_device_uninit(adev) at L191 invokes put_device(&adev->dev) on a device whose refcount is already 0, triggering a refcount_t underflow (use-after-free warning) and a recursive release path. kfree(adev->name) at L193 and kfree(adev) at L194 then operate on memory that is in a corrupted or already-freed state, constituting UAF and double-free. The error path in devm_adev_add (L251) is equally affected: auxiliary_device_uninit called on add-failure drops the init ref, fires adev_release, which calls auxiliary_device_uninit again.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An admin-level trigger such as sysfs unbind, PECI device hot-unplug, or driver unload invokes devm cleanup, which calls unregister_adev -> auxiliary_device_delete(adev); auxiliary_device_delete drops the init reference, refcount hits 0, adev_release executes, calls auxiliary_device_uninit/put_device a second time causing refcount underflow and freeing the kobject, then kfree(adev->name) and kfree(adev) operate on freed memory—exploitable for kernel heap corruption.
